qa_pairs.question: What is the command to load the default version of a software package on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: To load the default version of a software package, you can use the command `module load <module-name>`.

qa_pairs.question: How do I load a specific version of a software package using modules?
qa_pairs.answer: You can load a specific version of a software package by using the command `module load <module-name>/<module-version>`.

qa_pairs.question: What command should I use to unload all currently loaded modules?
qa_pairs.answer: To unload all currently loaded modules, execute the command `module purge`.

qa_pairs.question: How can I see a list of all available modules that can be loaded?
qa_pairs.answer: You can list all available modules by using the command `module avail`.

qa_pairs.question: Which command allows me to view the modules I currently have loaded?
qa_pairs.answer: To show your currently loaded modules, use the command `module list`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the command for searching modules and their versions?
qa_pairs.answer: You can search for modules and their versions using `module spider` or `module spider <module-name>`.
